3	import (
4		"context"

14		"github.com/nextlevelbuilder/goclaw/internal/store"
15	)
16	
17	// Compile-time interface assertions.
18	var (
19		_ channels.Channel           = (*Channel)(nil)
20		_ channels.WebhookChannel    = (*Channel)(nil)
21		_ channels.BlockReplyChannel = (*Channel)(nil)
22	)
23	
24	const (
25		dedupTTL        = 24 * time.Hour
26		dedupCleanEvery = 5 * time.Minute
27		outboundEchoTTL = 45 * time.Second
28	)
29	
30	// Channel implements channels.Channel and channels.WebhookChannel for Pancake (pages.fm).
31	// One channel instance = one Pancake page, which may serve multiple platforms (FB, Zalo, IG, etc.)
32	type Channel struct {
33		*channels.BaseChannel
34		config        pancakeInstanceConfig
35		apiClient     *APIClient
36		pageID        string
37		pageName      string // resolved from Pancake page metadata at Start()
38		platform      string // resolved from Pancake page metadata at Start()
39		webhookSecret string // optional HMAC-SHA256 secret for webhook verification
40	
41		// dedup prevents processing duplicate webhook deliveries.
42		dedup sync.Map // eventKey(string) → time.Time
43	
44		// recentOutbound suppresses short-lived webhook echoes of our own text replies.
45		recentOutbound sync.Map // conversationID + "\x00" + normalized content → time.Time
46	
47		stopCh  chan struct{}
48		stopCtx context.Context
49		stopFn  context.CancelFunc
50	}
51	
52	// New creates a Pancake Channel from parsed credentials and config.
53	func New(cfg pancakeInstanceConfig, creds pancakeCreds,
54		msgBus *bus.MessageBus, _ store.PairingStore) (*Channel, error) {
55	
56		if creds.APIKey == "" {
57			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pancake: api_key is required")
58		}
59		if creds.PageAccessToken == "" {
60			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pancake: page_access_token is required")
61		}
62		if cfg.PageID == "" {
63			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pancake: page_id is required")
64		}
65	
66		base := channels.NewBaseChannel(channels.TypePancake, msgBus, cfg.AllowFrom)
67		stopCtx, stopFn := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
68	
69		ch := &Channel{
70			BaseChannel:   base,
71			config:        cfg,
72			apiClient:     NewAPIClient(creds.APIKey, creds.PageAccessToken, cfg.PageID),
73			pageID:        cfg.PageID,
74			platform:      cfg.Platform,
75			webhookSecret: creds.WebhookSecret,
76			stopCh:        make(chan struct{}),
77			stopCtx:       stopCtx,
78			stopFn:        stopFn,
79		}
80	
81		return ch, nil
82	}
83	
84	// Factory creates a Pancake Channel from DB instance data.
85	// Implements channels.ChannelFactory.
86	func Factory(name string, creds json.RawMessage, cfg json.RawMessage,
87		msgBus *bus.MessageBus, pairingSvc store.PairingStore) (channels.Channel, error) {
88	
89		var c pancakeCreds
90		if err := json.Unmarshal(creds, &c); err != nil {
91			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pancake: decode credentials: %w", err)
92		}
93	
94		var ic pancakeInstanceConfig
95		if len(cfg) > 0 {
96			if err := json.Unmarshal(cfg, &ic); err != nil {
97				return nil, fmt.Errorf("pancake: decode config: %w", err)
98			}
99		}
100	
101		ch, err := New(ic, c, msgBus, pairingSvc)
102		if err != nil {
103			return nil, err
104		}
105		ch.SetName(name)
106		return ch, nil
107	}
108	
109	// Start connects the channel: verifies token, resolves platform, registers webhook.
110	func (ch *Channel) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
111		ch.MarkStarting("connecting to Pancake page")
112	
113		if err := ch.apiClient.VerifyToken(ctx); err != nil {
114			ch.MarkFailed("token invalid", err.Error(), channels.ChannelFailureKindAuth, false)
115			return err
116		}
117	
118		// Resolve platform and page name from page metadata (best-effort — don't fail on this).
119		if ch.platform == "" || ch.pageName == "" {
120			if page, err := ch.apiClient.GetPage(ctx); err != nil {
121				slog.Warn("pancake: could not resolve platform from page metadata", "page_id", ch.pageID, "err", err)
122			} else {
123				if page.Platform != "" {
124					ch.platform = page.Platform
125				}
126				if page.Name != "" {
127					ch.pageName = page.Name
128				}
129			}
130		}
131	
132		if ch.webhookSecret == "" {
133			slog.Warn("security.pancake_webhook_no_secret",
134				"page_id", ch.pageID,
135				"note", "webhook_secret not configured; incoming webhook requests will not be authenticated")
136		}
137	
138		globalRouter.register(ch)
139		ch.MarkHealthy("connected to page " + ch.pageID)
140		ch.SetRunning(true)
141	
142		// Background goroutine: evict stale dedup entries to prevent memory growth.
143		go ch.runDedupCleaner()
144	
145		slog.Info("pancake channel started",
146			"page_id", ch.pageID,
147			"platform", ch.platform,
148			"name", ch.Name())
149		return nil
150	}
151	
152	// Stop gracefully shuts down the channel.
153	func (ch *Channel) Stop(_ context.Context) error {
154		globalRouter.unregister(ch.pageID)
155		ch.stopFn()
156		close(ch.stopCh)
157		ch.SetRunning(false)
158		ch.MarkStopped("stopped")
159		slog.Info("pancake channel stopped", "page_id", ch.pageID, "name", ch.Name())
160		return nil
161	}
162	
163	// Send delivers an outbound message via Pancake API.
164	func (ch *Channel) Send(ctx context.Context, msg bus.OutboundMessage) error {
165		slog.Debug("pancake: Send called",
166			"page_id", ch.pageID,
167			"chat_id", msg.ChatID,
168			"content_len", len(msg.Content),
169			"platform", ch.platform,
170			"channel_name", ch.Name())
171	
172		conversationID := msg.ChatID
173		if conversationID == "" {
174			return fmt.Errorf("pancake: chat_id (conversation_id) is required for outbound message")
175		}
176	
177		text := FormatOutbound(msg.Content, ch.platform)
178	
179		// Handle media attachments.
180		attachmentIDs, err := ch.handleMediaAttachments(ctx, msg)
181		if err != nil {
182			slog.Warn("pancake: media upload failed, sending text only",
183				"page_id", ch.pageID, "err", err)
184		}
185	
186		// Pancake's official contract makes `message` and `content_ids` mutually exclusive.
187		// Deliver uploaded media first, then follow with text chunks if needed.
188		if len(attachmentIDs) > 0 {
189			if err := ch.apiClient.SendAttachmentMessage(ctx, conversationID, attachmentIDs); err != nil {
190				ch.handleAPIError(err)
191				return err
192			}
193			if text == "" {
194				return nil
195			}
196		}
197	
198		// Text-only: split into platform-appropriate chunks.
199		// Store echo fingerprints BEFORE sending so that webhook echoes arriving
200		// while the HTTP round-trip is in flight are already recognized as self-sent.
201		parts := splitMessage(text, ch.maxMessageLength())
202		for _, part := range parts {
203			ch.rememberOutboundEcho(conversationID, part)
204		}
205		for _, part := range parts {
206			if err := ch.apiClient.SendMessage(ctx, conversationID, part); err != nil {
207				ch.handleAPIError(err)
208				// Remove pre-stored echo fingerprints for unsent parts so they
209				// don't suppress genuine customer messages that happen to match.
210				ch.forgetOutboundEcho(conversationID, part)
211				return err
212			}
213		}
214		return nil
215	}
216	
217	// BlockReplyEnabled returns the per-channel block_reply override (nil = inherit gateway default).
218	func (ch *Channel) BlockReplyEnabled() *bool { return ch.config.BlockReply }
219	
220	// WebhookHandler returns the shared webhook path and global router as handler.
221	// Only the first pancake instance mounts the route; others return ("", nil).
222	func (ch *Channel) WebhookHandler() (string, http.Handler) {
223		return globalRouter.webhookRoute()
224	}
225	
226	// handleAPIError maps Pancake API errors to channel health states.
227	func (ch *Channel) handleAPIError(err error) {
228		if err == nil {
229			return
230		}
231		switch {
232		case isAuthError(err):
233			ch.MarkFailed("token expired or invalid", err.Error(), channels.ChannelFailureKindAuth, false)
234		case isRateLimitError(err):
235			ch.MarkDegraded("rate limited", err.Error(), channels.ChannelFailureKindNetwork, true)
236		default:
237			ch.MarkDegraded("api error", err.Error(), channels.ChannelFailureKindUnknown, true)
238		}
239	}
240	
241	// maxMessageLength returns the platform-specific character limit.
242	func (ch *Channel) maxMessageLength() int {
243		switch ch.platform {
244		case "tiktok":
245			return 500
246		case "instagram":
247			return 1000
248		case "facebook", "zalo":
249			return 2000
250		case "whatsapp":
251			return 4096
252		case "line":
253			return 5000
254		default:
255			return 2000
256		}
257	}

1	// Package pancake implements the Pancake (pages.fm) channel for GoClaw.
2	// Pancake acts as a unified proxy for Facebook, Zalo OA, Instagram, TikTok, WhatsApp, Line.
3	// A single Pancake API key gives access to all connected platforms — no per-platform OAuth needed.
4	package pancake
5	
6	import "encoding/json"
7	
8	// pancakeCreds holds encrypted credentials stored in channel_instances.credentials.
9	type pancakeCreds struct {
10		APIKey          string `json:"api_key"`                  // User-level Pancake API key
11		PageAccessToken string `json:"page_access_token"`        // Page-level token for all page APIs
12		WebhookSecret   string `json:"webhook_secret,omitempty"` // Optional HMAC-SHA256 verification
13	}
14	
15	// pancakeInstanceConfig holds non-secret config from channel_instances.config JSONB.
16	type pancakeInstanceConfig struct {
17		PageID   string `json:"page_id"`
18		Platform string `json:"platform,omitempty"` // auto-detected at Start(): facebook/zalo/instagram/tiktok/whatsapp/line
19		Features struct {
20			InboxReply   bool `json:"inbox_reply"`
21			CommentReply bool `json:"comment_reply"`
22		} `json:"features"`
23		AllowFrom  []string `json:"allow_from,omitempty"`
24		BlockReply *bool    `json:"block_reply,omitempty"` // override gateway block_reply (nil = inherit)
25	}
26	
27	// --- Webhook payload types ---
28	// These types match the actual Pancake (pages.fm) webhook delivery format.
29	// Top-level envelope has optional "event_type" + nested "data" containing
30	// "conversation" and "message" objects.
31	
32	// WebhookEvent is the top-level Pancake webhook delivery envelope.
33	type WebhookEvent struct {
34		EventType string          `json:"event_type,omitempty"` // "messaging", may be empty
35		PageID    string          `json:"page_id,omitempty"`    // top-level page_id (some formats)
36		Data      json.RawMessage `json:"data"`
37	}
38	
39	// WebhookData is the "data" envelope inside a Pancake webhook event.
40	type WebhookData struct {
41		Conversation WebhookConversation `json:"conversation"`
42		Message      WebhookMessage      `json:"message"`
43		PageID       string              `json:"page_id,omitempty"` // page_id may appear here or top-level
44	}
45	
46	// WebhookConversation holds the conversation metadata from a Pancake webhook.
47	type WebhookConversation struct {
48		ID          string        `json:"id"`                    // format: "pageID_senderID"
49		Type        string        `json:"type"`                  // "INBOX" or "COMMENT"
50		AssigneeIDs []string      `json:"assignee_ids,omitempty"` // Pancake staff IDs assigned to this conversation
51		From        WebhookSender `json:"from"`
52		Snippet     string        `json:"snippet,omitempty"`
53	}
54	
55	// WebhookSender identifies the message sender.
56	type WebhookSender struct {
57		ID             string `json:"id"`
58		Name           string `json:"name"`
59		Email          string `json:"email,omitempty"`
60		PageCustomerID string `json:"page_customer_id,omitempty"`
61	}
62	
63	// WebhookMessage holds the message payload from a Pancake webhook.
64	type WebhookMessage struct {
65		ID              string              `json:"id"`
66		Message         string              `json:"message,omitempty"`          // primary text content
67		OriginalMessage string              `json:"original_message,omitempty"` // unformatted fallback
68		Content         string              `json:"content,omitempty"`          // legacy field
69		From            *WebhookSender      `json:"from,omitempty"`             // actual sender of this message
70		Attachments     []MessageAttachment `json:"attachments,omitempty"`
71		CreatedAt       json.Number         `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
72	}
73	
74	// MessageAttachment represents a media attachment in a Pancake webhook message.
75	type MessageAttachment struct {
76		Type string `json:"type"` // "image", "video", "file"
77		URL  string `json:"url"`
78	}
79	
80	// MessagingData is the normalized internal representation used after parsing.
81	type MessagingData struct {
82		PageID         string
83		ConversationID string
84		Type           string // "INBOX" or "COMMENT"
85		Platform       string // "facebook", "zalo", "instagram", "tiktok", "whatsapp", "line"
86		AssigneeIDs    []string
87		Message        MessagingMessage
88	}
89	
90	// MessagingMessage is the normalized message used by the handler.
91	type MessagingMessage struct {
92		ID          string
93		Content     string
94		SenderID    string
95		SenderName  string
96		Attachments []MessageAttachment
97	}
98	
99	// --- API response types ---
100	
101	// PageInfo holds page metadata from GET /pages response.
102	type PageInfo struct {
103		ID       string `json:"id"`
104		Name     string `json:"name"`
105		Platform string `json:"platform"` // facebook/zalo/instagram/tiktok/whatsapp/line
106		Avatar   string `json:"avatar,omitempty"`
107	}
108	
109	// SendMessageRequest is the POST body for sending a message via Pancake API.
110	type SendMessageRequest struct {
111		Action     string   `json:"action"`
112		Message    string   `json:"message,omitempty"`
113		ContentIDs []string `json:"content_ids,omitempty"`
114	}
115	
116	// UploadResponse is returned by POST /pages/{id}/upload_contents.
117	type UploadResponse struct {
118		ID  string `json:"id"`
119		URL string `json:"url,omitempty"`
120	}
121	
122	// apiError wraps a Pancake API error response.
123	type apiError struct {
124		Code    int    `json:"code"`
125		Message string `json:"message"`
126	}
127	
128	func (e *apiError) Error() string {
129		return e.Message
130	}

1	package pancake
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/hmac"
5		"crypto/sha256"
6		"encoding/hex"
7		"encoding/json"
8		"io"
9		"log/slog"
10		"net/http"
11		"strings"
12		"sync"
13	)
14	
15	const (
16		webhookPath  = "/channels/pancake/webhook"
17		maxBodyBytes = 1 << 20 // 1 MB — prevent abuse
18	)
19	
20	// verifyHMAC verifies a Pancake HMAC-SHA256 signature.
21	// Expected header format: "sha256=<hex-digest>"
22	func verifyHMAC(body []byte, secret, signature string) bool {
23		const prefix = "sha256="
24		if len(signature) <= len(prefix) {
25			return false
26		}
27		got, err := hex.DecodeString(signature[len(prefix):])
28		if err != nil {
29			return false
30		}
31		mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
32		mac.Write(body)
33		expected := mac.Sum(nil)
34		return hmac.Equal(got, expected)
35	}
36	
37	// --- Global webhook router for multi-page support ---
38	
39	// webhookRouter routes incoming Pancake webhook events to the correct channel instance by page_id.
40	// A single HTTP handler is shared across all pancake channel instances.
41	type webhookRouter struct {
42		mu           sync.RWMutex
43		instances    map[string]*Channel // pageID → channel
44		routeHandled bool                // true after first webhookRoute() call
45	}
46	
47	var globalRouter = &webhookRouter{
48		instances: make(map[string]*Channel),
49	}
50	
51	func (r *webhookRouter) register(ch *Channel) {
52		r.mu.Lock()
53		defer r.mu.Unlock()
54		r.instances[ch.pageID] = ch
55	}
56	
57	func (r *webhookRouter) unregister(pageID string) {
58		r.mu.Lock()
59		defer r.mu.Unlock()
60		delete(r.instances, pageID)
61	}
62	
63	// webhookRoute returns the path+handler on first call; ("", nil) for subsequent calls.
64	// The HTTP mux retains the route once registered — routeHandled prevents duplicate mounts.
65	func (r *webhookRouter) webhookRoute() (string, http.Handler) {
66		r.mu.Lock()
67		defer r.mu.Unlock()
68		if !r.routeHandled {
69			r.routeHandled = true
70			return webhookPath, r
71		}
72		return "", nil
73	}
74	
75	// ServeHTTP is the shared handler for all Pancake page webhooks.
76	// Always returns HTTP 200 — Pancake suspends webhooks if >80% errors in a 30-min window.
77	func (r *webhookRouter) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
78		slog.Info("pancake: webhook request received",
79			"method", req.Method,
80			"remote_addr", req.RemoteAddr,
81			"content_length", req.ContentLength)
82	
83		if req.Method != http.MethodPost {
84			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
85			return
86		}
87	
88		lr := io.LimitReader(req.Body, maxBodyBytes+1)
89		body, err := io.ReadAll(lr)
90		if err != nil {
91			slog.Warn("pancake: router read body error", "err", err)
92			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
93			return
94		}
95	
96		slog.Debug("pancake: webhook body received",
97			"body_len", len(body),
98			"body_preview", truncateBody(body, 1000))
99	
100		// Parse top-level envelope.
101		var event WebhookEvent
102		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &event); err != nil {
103			slog.Warn("pancake: router parse event error", "err", err, "body_preview", truncateBody(body, 300))
104			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
105			return
106		}
107	
108		// Parse the nested "data" object containing conversation + message.
109		var data WebhookData
110		if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data, &data); err != nil {
111			slog.Warn("pancake: router parse data error", "err", err)
112			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
113			return
114		}
115	
116		// Resolve page_id: try top-level first, then data-level, then extract from conversation ID.
117		pageID := event.PageID
118		if pageID == "" {
119			pageID = data.PageID
120		}
121		if pageID == "" {
122			// Conversation ID format: "pageID_senderID" — extract page portion.
123			if idx := strings.Index(data.Conversation.ID, "_"); idx > 0 {
124				pageID = data.Conversation.ID[:idx]
125			}
126		}
127	
128		// Resolve conversation type — only process INBOX messages.
129		convType := strings.ToUpper(data.Conversation.Type)
130	
131		if event.EventType != "" && !strings.EqualFold(event.EventType, "messaging") {
132			slog.Debug("pancake: skipping non-messaging webhook event",
133				"event_type", event.EventType,
134				"page_id", pageID)
135			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
136			return
137		}
138	
139		slog.Debug("pancake: webhook event parsed",
140			"event_type", event.EventType,
141			"page_id", pageID,
142			"conv_id", data.Conversation.ID,
143			"conv_type", convType,
144			"sender_id", data.Conversation.From.ID,
145			"sender_name", data.Conversation.From.Name,
146			"msg_id", data.Message.ID)
147	
148		if pageID == "" {
149			slog.Warn("pancake: could not determine page_id from webhook payload")
150			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
151			return
152		}
153		if convType != "INBOX" {
154			slog.Info("pancake: skipping non-inbox conversation event",
155				"page_id", pageID,
156				"conv_id", data.Conversation.ID,
157				"conv_type", convType,
158				"msg_id", data.Message.ID)
159			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
160			return
161		}
162	
163		r.mu.RLock()
164		target := r.instances[pageID]
165		r.mu.RUnlock()
166	
167		if target == nil {
168			// Log all registered page IDs for debugging.
169			r.mu.RLock()
170			var registered []string
171			for pid := range r.instances {
172				registered = append(registered, pid)
173			}
174			r.mu.RUnlock()
175			slog.Warn("pancake: no channel instance for page_id",
176				"page_id", pageID,
177				"registered_pages", registered)
178			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
179			return
180		}
181	
182		// HMAC signature verification — skip if webhook_secret not configured.
183		if target.webhookSecret != "" {
184			sig := req.Header.Get("X-Pancake-Signature")
185			if !verifyHMAC(body, target.webhookSecret, sig) {
186				slog.Warn("security.pancake_webhook_signature_mismatch",
187					"page_id", pageID,
188					"remote_addr", req.RemoteAddr)
189				w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
190				return
191			}
192		}
193	
194		// Build normalized MessagingData from actual Pancake payload.
195		msgContent := data.Message.Message
196		if msgContent == "" {
197			msgContent = data.Message.OriginalMessage
198		}
199		if msgContent == "" {
200			msgContent = data.Message.Content
201		}
202		if msgContent == "" {
203			msgContent = data.Conversation.Snippet
204		}
205	
206		senderID := data.Conversation.From.ID
207		senderName := data.Conversation.From.Name
208		if data.Message.From != nil {
209			if data.Message.From.ID != "" {
210				senderID = data.Message.From.ID
211			}
212			if data.Message.From.Name != "" {
213				senderName = data.Message.From.Name
214			}
215		}
216	
217		normalized := MessagingData{
218			PageID:         pageID,
219			ConversationID: data.Conversation.ID,
220			Type:           convType,
221			Platform:       target.platform,
222			AssigneeIDs:    append([]string(nil), data.Conversation.AssigneeIDs...),
223			Message: MessagingMessage{
224				ID:          data.Message.ID,
225				Content:     msgContent,
226				SenderID:    senderID,
227				SenderName:  senderName,
228				Attachments: data.Message.Attachments,
229			},
230		}
231	
232		target.handleMessagingEvent(normalized)
233		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
234	}
235	
236	// truncateBody returns a string preview of body, truncated to maxLen bytes.
237	func truncateBody(body []byte, maxLen int) string {
238		if len(body) <= maxLen {
239			return string(body)
240		}
241		return string(body[:maxLen]) + "..."
242	}
243

1	package pancake
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"html"
6		"log/slog"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/nextlevelbuilder/goclaw/internal/channels"
10	)
11	
12	// handleMessagingEvent converts a Pancake "messaging" webhook event to bus.InboundMessage.
13	func (ch *Channel) handleMessagingEvent(data MessagingData) {
14		slog.Debug("pancake: handleMessagingEvent called",
15			"page_id", ch.pageID,
16			"sender_id", data.Message.SenderID,
17			"conversation_id", data.ConversationID,
18			"type", data.Type,
19			"platform", data.Platform,
20			"msg_id", data.Message.ID,
21			"content_len", len(data.Message.Content))
22	
23		// Dedup by message ID to handle Pancake's at-least-once delivery.
24		// Skip dedup when message ID is empty — prevents shared slot "msg:" from
25		// silently dropping all subsequent empty-ID messages across conversations.
26		var dedupKey string
27		if data.Message.ID != "" {
28			dedupKey = fmt.Sprintf("msg:%s", data.Message.ID)
29			if ch.isDup(dedupKey) {
30				slog.Info("pancake: duplicate message skipped", "msg_id", data.Message.ID)
31				return
32			}
33		}
34	
35		// Prevent reply loops: skip messages sent by the page itself.
36		if data.Message.SenderID == ch.pageID {
37			slog.Info("pancake: skipping own page message",
38				"page_id", ch.pageID,
39				"sender_id", data.Message.SenderID)
40			return
41		}
42		if isAssignedStaff(data.AssigneeIDs, data.Message.SenderID) {
43			slog.Info("pancake: skipping assigned staff message",
44				"page_id", ch.pageID,
45				"sender_id", data.Message.SenderID,
46				"conversation_id", data.ConversationID)
47			return
48		}
49	
50		if data.Message.SenderID == "" {
51			slog.Warn("pancake: message missing sender_id, skipping", "msg_id", data.Message.ID)
52			return
53		}
54	
55		// Check echo BEFORE buildMessageContent adds the [From: ...] prefix.
56		// rememberOutboundEcho stores the raw outbound text; the prefix would cause a
57		// key mismatch and silently break loop detection.
58		if ch.isRecentOutboundEcho(data.ConversationID, data.Message.Content) {
59			slog.Info("pancake: skipping recent outbound echo",
60				"page_id", ch.pageID,
61				"conversation_id", data.ConversationID,
62				"msg_id", data.Message.ID)
63			return
64		}
65	
66		content := buildMessageContent(data)
67	
68		metadata := map[string]string{
69			"pancake_mode":      strings.ToLower(data.Type), // "inbox" or "comment"
70			"conversation_type": data.Type,
71			"platform":          data.Platform,
72			"conversation_id":   data.ConversationID,
73			"message_id":        dedupKey,
74			"display_name":      channels.SanitizeDisplayName(data.Message.SenderName),
75			"page_name":         ch.pageName,
76		}
77	
78		ch.HandleMessage(
79			data.Message.SenderID,
80			data.ConversationID, // ChatID = conversation_id for reply routing
81			content,
82			nil, // media handled inline via content URLs
83			metadata,
84			"direct", // Pancake inbox conversations are always treated as direct messages
85		)
86	
87		slog.Debug("pancake: inbound message published to bus",
88			"page_id", ch.pageID,
89			"conv_id", data.ConversationID,
90			"sender_id", data.Message.SenderID,
91			"platform", data.Platform,
92			"type", data.Type,
93			"channel_name", ch.Name(),
94		)
95	}
96	
97	// buildMessageContent combines text content and attachment URLs into a single string.
98	// Format: [From: {SenderID} ({SenderName})] {content}
99	func buildMessageContent(data MessagingData) string {
100		parts := []string{}
101	
102		if data.Message.Content != "" {
103			parts = append(parts, stripHTML(data.Message.Content))
104		}
105	
106		for _, att := range data.Message.Attachments {
107			if att.URL != "" {
108				parts = append(parts, att.URL)
109			}
110		}
111	
112		body := strings.Join(parts, "\n")
113	
114		if data.Message.SenderID != "" {
115			prefix := fmt.Sprintf("[From: %s (%s)]", data.Message.SenderID, data.Message.SenderName)
116			if body != "" {
117				return prefix + " " + body
118			}
119			return prefix
120		}
121		return body
122	}
123	
124	// stripHTML removes HTML tags and unescapes HTML entities from s.
125	func stripHTML(s string) string {
126		s = htmlTagRe.ReplaceAllString(s, "")
127		return html.UnescapeString(strings.TrimSpace(s))
128	}
129	
130	func isAssignedStaff(assigneeIDs []string, senderID string) bool {
131		if senderID == "" {
132			return false
133		}
134		for _, assigneeID := range assigneeIDs {
135			if assigneeID == senderID {
136				return true
137			}
138		}
139		return false
140	}
141

1	// Package facebook implements the Facebook Fanpage channel for GoClaw.
2	// Supports: comment auto-reply, Messenger inbox auto-reply, first inbox DM.
3	package facebook
4	
5	// facebookCreds holds encrypted credentials stored in channel_instances.credentials.
6	type facebookCreds struct {
7		PageAccessToken string `json:"page_access_token"`
8		AppSecret       string `json:"app_secret"`
9		VerifyToken     string `json:"verify_token"`
10	}
11	
12	// facebookInstanceConfig holds non-secret config from channel_instances.config JSONB.
13	type facebookInstanceConfig struct {
14		PageID   string   `json:"page_id"`
15		Features struct {
16			CommentReply       bool `json:"comment_reply"`
17			FirstInbox         bool `json:"first_inbox"`
18			MessengerAutoReply bool `json:"messenger_auto_reply"`
19		} `json:"features"`
20		CommentReplyOptions struct {
21			IncludePostContext bool `json:"include_post_context"`
22			MaxThreadDepth     int  `json:"max_thread_depth"`
23		} `json:"comment_reply_options"`
24		MessengerOptions struct {
25			SessionTimeout string `json:"session_timeout"`
26		} `json:"messenger_options"`
27		PostContextCacheTTL string   `json:"post_context_cache_ttl"`
28		// FirstInboxMessage is the DM text sent to commenters (first-inbox feature).
29		// Defaults to Vietnamese if empty. Operators should set this to match their page language.
30		FirstInboxMessage string   `json:"first_inbox_message,omitempty"`
31		AllowFrom         []string `json:"allow_from,omitempty"`
32	}
33	
34	// --- Webhook payloads ---
35	
36	// WebhookPayload is the top-level Facebook webhook event payload.
37	type WebhookPayload struct {
38		Object string         `json:"object"`
39		Entry  []WebhookEntry `json:"entry"`
40	}
41	
42	// WebhookEntry is one page's events within a webhook delivery.
43	type WebhookEntry struct {
44		ID        string           `json:"id"`   // page_id
45		Time      int64            `json:"time"`
46		Changes   []WebhookChange  `json:"changes,omitempty"`   // feed events (comments, posts)
47		Messaging []MessagingEvent `json:"messaging,omitempty"` // Messenger events
48	}
49	
50	// WebhookChange is a single change event for feed subscriptions.
51	type WebhookChange struct {
52		Field string      `json:"field"` // "feed", "mention", etc.
53		Value ChangeValue `json:"value"`
54	}
55	
56	// ChangeValue holds the details of a feed change event.
57	type ChangeValue struct {
58		From        FBUser `json:"from"`
59		Item        string `json:"item"`      // "comment", "post", "status"
60		CommentID   string `json:"comment_id"`
61		PostID      string `json:"post_id"`
62		ParentID    string `json:"parent_id"` // parent comment ID for nested replies
63		Message     string `json:"message"`
64		Verb        string `json:"verb"`        // "add", "edit", "remove"
65		CreatedTime int64  `json:"created_time"`
66	}
67	
68	// MessagingEvent is a single Messenger inbox event.
69	type MessagingEvent struct {
70		Sender    FBUser           `json:"sender"`
71		Recipient FBUser           `json:"recipient"`
72		Timestamp int64            `json:"timestamp"`
73		Message   *IncomingMessage `json:"message,omitempty"`

22	// processNormalMessage handles routing, scheduling, and response delivery for a single
23	// (possibly merged) inbound message. Called directly by the debouncer's flush callback.
24	func processNormalMessage(
25		ctx context.Context,
26		msg bus.InboundMessage,
27		deps *ConsumerDeps,
28	) {
29		// Inject tenant from channel instance into context so all store operations
30		// (agent lookup, session creation, etc.) are tenant-scoped.
31		if msg.TenantID != uuid.Nil {
32			ctx = store.WithTenantID(ctx, msg.TenantID)
33		} else {
34			ctx = store.WithTenantID(ctx, store.MasterTenantID)
35		}
36	
37		// Determine target agent via bindings or explicit AgentID
38		agentID := msg.AgentID
39		if agentID == "" {
40			agentID = resolveAgentRoute(deps.Cfg, msg.Channel, msg.ChatID, msg.PeerKind)
41		}
42	
43		agentLoop, err := deps.Agents.Get(ctx, agentID)
44		if err != nil {
45			slog.Warn("inbound: agent not found", "agent", agentID, "channel", msg.Channel)
46			return
47		}
48	
49		// Build session key based on scope config (matching TS buildAgentPeerSessionKey).
50		peerKind := msg.PeerKind
51		if peerKind == "" {
52			peerKind = string(sessions.PeerDirect) // default to DM
53		}
54		sessionKey := sessions.BuildScopedSessionKey(agentID, msg.Channel, sessions.PeerKind(peerKind), msg.ChatID)
55	
56		// Thread-based isolation override (e.g. Slack DM threads, AI Panel)
57		if lk := msg.Metadata["local_key"]; lk != "" && strings.Contains(lk, ":thread:") {
58			parts := strings.SplitN(lk, ":thread:", 2)
59			if len(parts) == 2 {
60				sessionKey = sessions.BuildScopedThreadSessionKey(agentID, msg.Channel, sessions.PeerKind(peerKind), msg.ChatID, parts[1])
61			}
62		}
63	
64		// Forum topic: override session key to isolate per-topic history.
65		// TS ref: buildTelegramGroupPeerId() in src/telegram/bot/helpers.ts
66		if msg.Metadata[tools.MetaIsForum] == "true" && peerKind == string(sessions.PeerGroup) {
67			var topicID int
68			fmt.Sscanf(msg.Metadata[tools.MetaMessageThreadID], "%d", &topicID)
69			if topicID > 0 {
70				sessionKey = sessions.BuildGroupTopicSessionKey(agentID, msg.Channel, msg.ChatID, topicID)
71			}
72		}
73	
74		// DM thread: override session key to isolate per-thread history in private chats.
75		if msg.Metadata[tools.MetaDMThreadID] != "" && peerKind == string(sessions.PeerDirect) {
76			var threadID int
77			fmt.Sscanf(msg.Metadata[tools.MetaDMThreadID], "%d", &threadID)
78			if threadID > 0 {
79				sessionKey = sessions.BuildDMThreadSessionKey(agentID, msg.Channel, msg.ChatID, threadID)
80			}
81		}
82	
83		// Group-scoped UserID: context files, memory, traces, and seeding scope.
84		// - Discord guilds: "guild:{guildID}:user:{senderID}" — per-user per-server,
85		//   shared across all channels within the same server. Session key stays per-channel.
86		// - Other platforms: "group:{channel}:{chatID}" — shared by all users in the chat.
87		// Individual senderID is preserved in InboundMessage for pairing/dedup/mention gate.
88		userID := msg.UserID
89		if peerKind == string(sessions.PeerGroup) && msg.ChatID != "" {
90			if guildID := msg.Metadata["guild_id"]; guildID != "" && msg.SenderID != "" {
91				// Discord guild: per-user scope so each member has own profile
92				// across all channels in the same server.
93				userID = fmt.Sprintf("guild:%s:user:%s", guildID, msg.SenderID)
94			} else {
95				groupID := msg.ChatID
96				userID = fmt.Sprintf("group:%s:%s", msg.Channel, groupID)
97			}
98		}
99	
100		// Persist friendly names from channel metadata into session + user profile.
101		sessionMeta := extractSessionMetadata(msg, peerKind)
102		if len(sessionMeta) > 0 {
103			deps.SessStore.SetSessionMetadata(ctx, sessionKey, sessionMeta)
104			if deps.AgentStore != nil {
105				if agentUUID, err := uuid.Parse(agentID); err == nil && agentUUID != uuid.Nil {
106					_ = deps.AgentStore.UpdateUserProfileMetadata(ctx, agentUUID, userID, sessionMeta)
107				}
108			}
109		}
110	
111		// Set session label for Pancake channels: "Pancake:{SenderName}:{PageName}"
112		if msg.Metadata["pancake_mode"] != "" {
113			label := buildPancakeSessionLabel(msg.Metadata["display_name"], msg.Metadata["page_name"])
114			deps.SessStore.SetLabel(ctx, sessionKey, label)
115		}
116	
117		// Auto-collect channel contacts for the contact selector.
118		// Skip internal senders (system:*, notification:*, teammate:*, ticker:*, session_send_tool).
119		if deps.ContactCollector != nil && msg.SenderID != "" && !bus.IsInternalSender(msg.SenderID) {
120			senderNumericID := msg.SenderID
121			if idx := strings.IndexByte(senderNumericID, '|'); idx > 0 {
122				senderNumericID = senderNumericID[:idx]
123			}
124			channelType := deps.ChannelMgr.ChannelTypeForName(msg.Channel)
125			if channelType == "" {
126				channelType = msg.Channel // fallback to instance name
127			}
128			displayName := sessionMeta["display_name"]
129			username := sessionMeta["username"]
130			deps.ContactCollector.EnsureContact(ctx, channelType, msg.Channel, senderNumericID, userID, displayName, username, peerKind, "user", "", "")
131	
132			// Also collect group chat as a contact (for group permission management / merge).
133			// Group IDs (e.g., Telegram "-100456") differ from user IDs — no UNIQUE conflict.
134			if peerKind == string(sessions.PeerGroup) && msg.ChatID != "" {
135				groupTitle := msg.Metadata[tools.MetaChatTitle] // Telegram: message.Chat.Title
136				deps.ContactCollector.EnsureContact(ctx, channelType, msg.Channel, msg.ChatID, "", groupTitle, "", "group", "group", "", "")
137			}
138		}
139	
140		// --- Resolve merged tenant user identity ---
141		// If the sender has been merged to a tenant_user, use the tenant user's ID
142		// for DM sessions. This enables per-user features (MCP creds, SecureCLI creds).
143		// Group sessions keep the group-scoped userID; sender resolution happens via SenderID.
144		if deps.ContactCollector != nil && peerKind == string(sessions.PeerDirect) && msg.SenderID != "" && !bus.IsInternalSender(msg.SenderID) {
145			senderNumeric := msg.SenderID
146			if idx := strings.IndexByte(senderNumeric, '|'); idx > 0 {
147				senderNumeric = senderNumeric[:idx]
148			}
149			chType := deps.ChannelMgr.ChannelTypeForName(msg.Channel)
150			if chType == "" {
151				chType = msg.Channel
152			}
153			if resolved, err := deps.ContactCollector.ResolveTenantUserID(ctx, chType, senderNumeric); err == nil && resolved != "" {
154				slog.Debug("contact.resolved_tenant_user", "sender", senderNumeric, "tenant_user", resolved)
155				userID = resolved
156			}
157		}
158	
159		// --- Quota check ---
160		if deps.QuotaChecker != nil {
161			qResult := deps.QuotaChecker.Check(ctx, userID, msg.Channel, agentLoop.ProviderName())
162			if !qResult.Allowed {
163				slog.Warn("security.quota_exceeded",
164					"user_id", userID,
165					"channel", msg.Channel,
166					"window", qResult.Window,
167					"used", qResult.Used,
168					"limit", qResult.Limit,
169				)
170				deps.MsgBus.PublishOutbound(bus.OutboundMessage{
171					Channel:  msg.Channel,
172					ChatID:   msg.ChatID,
173					Content:  formatQuotaExceeded(qResult),
174					Metadata: msg.Metadata,
175				})
176				return
177			}
178			deps.QuotaChecker.Increment(userID)
179		}
180	
181		// Auto-clear followup reminders when user sends a message on a real channel.
182		// Fire-and-forget: don't block message processing.
183		if deps.TeamStore != nil && msg.Channel != tools.ChannelSystem && msg.Channel != tools.ChannelTeammate && msg.Channel != tools.ChannelDashboard {
184			go func(ch, cid string) {
185				if n, err := deps.TeamStore.ClearFollowupByScope(ctx, ch, cid); err != nil {
186					slog.Warn("auto-clear followup failed", "channel", ch, "chat_id", cid, "error", err)
187				} else if n > 0 {
188					slog.Info("auto-clear followup: cleared", "channel", ch, "chat_id", cid, "count", n)
189				}
190			}(msg.Channel, msg.ChatID)
191		}
192	
193		slog.Info("inbound: scheduling message (main lane)",
194			"channel", msg.Channel,
195			"chat_id", msg.ChatID,
196			"peer_kind", peerKind,
197			"agent", agentID,
198			"session", sessionKey,
199			"user_id", userID,
200		)
201	
202		// Enable streaming when the channel supports it (so agent emits chunk events).
203		// The channel decides per chat type via separate dm_stream / group_stream flags.
204		isGroup := peerKind == string(sessions.PeerGroup)
205		enableStream := deps.ChannelMgr != nil && deps.ChannelMgr.IsStreamingChannel(msg.Channel, isGroup)
206	
207		// Group chats allow concurrent runs (multiple users can chat simultaneously).
208		maxConcurrent := 1
209		if peerKind == string(sessions.PeerGroup) {
210			maxConcurrent = 3
211		}
212	
213		runID := fmt.Sprintf("inbound-%s-%s-%s", msg.Channel, msg.ChatID, uuid.NewString()[:8])
214	
215		// Build outbound metadata for reply-to + thread routing BEFORE RegisterRun
216		// so block.reply handler can use it for routing intermediate messages.
217		outMeta := make(map[string]string)
218		if isGroup {
219			if mid := msg.Metadata["message_id"]; mid != "" {
220				outMeta["reply_to_message_id"] = mid
221			}
222		}
223		for _, k := range []string{tools.MetaMessageThreadID, "local_key", "placeholder_key", "group_id", "feishu_reply_target_id"} {
224			if v := msg.Metadata[k]; v != "" {
225				outMeta[k] = v
226			}
227		}
228	
229		// Register run with channel manager for streaming/reaction event forwarding.
230		// Use localKey (composite key with topic suffix) so streaming/reaction events
231		// route to the correct per-topic state in the channel.
232		messageID := msg.Metadata["message_id"]
233		chatIDForRun := msg.ChatID
234		if lk := msg.Metadata["local_key"]; lk != "" {
235			chatIDForRun = lk
236		}
237		blockReply := deps.ChannelMgr != nil && deps.ChannelMgr.ResolveBlockReply(msg.Channel, deps.Cfg.Gateway.BlockReply)
238		toolStatus := deps.Cfg.Gateway.ToolStatus == nil || *deps.Cfg.Gateway.ToolStatus // default true
239		if deps.ChannelMgr != nil {
240			deps.ChannelMgr.RegisterRun(runID, msg.Channel, chatIDForRun, messageID, outMeta, enableStream, blockReply, toolStatus)
241		}
242	
243		// Group-aware system prompt: help the LLM adapt tone and behavior for group chats.
244		var extraPrompt string
245		if peerKind == string(sessions.PeerGroup) {
246			extraPrompt = "You are in a GROUP chat (multiple participants), not a private 1-on-1 DM.\n" +
247				"- Messages may include a [Chat messages since your last reply] section with recent group history. Each history line shows \"sender [time]: message\".\n" +
248				"- The current message includes a [From: sender_name] tag identifying who @mentioned you.\n" +
249				"- Keep responses concise and focused; long replies are disruptive in groups.\n" +
250				"- Write like a human. Avoid Markdown tables. Use real line breaks sparingly.\n" +
251				"- Address the group naturally. If the history shows a multi-person conversation, consider the full context before answering."
252		}
253	
254		// Append per-topic system prompt (from group/topic config hierarchy).
255		if tsp := msg.Metadata[tools.MetaTopicSystemPrompt]; tsp != "" {
256			if extraPrompt != "" {
257				extraPrompt += "\n\n"
258			}
259			extraPrompt += tsp
260		}
261	
262		// Per-topic skill filter override (from group/topic config hierarchy).
263		var skillFilter []string
264		if ts := msg.Metadata[tools.MetaTopicSkills]; ts != "" {
265			skillFilter = strings.Split(ts, ",")
266		}
267	
268		// Delegation announces carry media as ForwardMedia (not deleted, forwarded to output).
269		// User-uploaded media goes in Media (loaded as images for LLM, then deleted).
270		var reqMedia, fwdMedia []bus.MediaFile
271		if msg.Metadata["delegation_id"] != "" || msg.Metadata["subagent_id"] != "" {
272			fwdMedia = msg.Media
273		} else {
274			reqMedia = msg.Media
275		}

277		// Intent classify fast-path: when agent is busy on DM, classify user intent
278		// to detect status queries, cancel requests, or steer/new_task for mid-run injection.
279		// Only for DM (maxConcurrent=1) where messages queue behind the active run.
280		if maxConcurrent == 1 && deps.Agents.IsSessionBusy(sessionKey) {
281			if loop, ok := agentLoop.(*agent.Loop); ok && loop.Provider() != nil {
282				locale := msg.Metadata["locale"]
283				if locale == "" {
284					locale = "en"
285				}
286				intent := agent.ClassifyIntent(ctx, loop.Provider(), loop.Model(), msg.Content)
287				switch intent {
288				case agent.IntentStatusQuery:
289					status := deps.Agents.GetActivity(sessionKey)
290					reply := agent.FormatStatusReply(status, locale)
291					deps.MsgBus.PublishOutbound(bus.OutboundMessage{
292						Channel:  msg.Channel,
293						ChatID:   msg.ChatID,
294						Content:  reply,
295						Metadata: outMeta,
296					})
297					return
298				case agent.IntentCancel:
299					aborted := deps.Agents.AbortRunsForSession(sessionKey)
300					if len(aborted) > 0 {
301						slog.Info("inbound: cancelled runs via intent classify",
302							"session", sessionKey, "aborted", aborted)
303						deps.MsgBus.PublishOutbound(bus.OutboundMessage{
304							Channel:  msg.Channel,
305							ChatID:   msg.ChatID,
306							Content:  i18n.T(locale, i18n.MsgCancelledReply),
307							Metadata: outMeta,
308						})
309					}
310					return
311				case agent.IntentSteer:
312					// Steer: inject into running loop to redirect/add to current task.
313					injected := deps.Agents.InjectMessage(sessionKey, agent.InjectedMessage{
314						Content: msg.Content,
315						UserID:  userID,
316					})
317					if injected {
318						slog.Info("inbound: injected steer message",
319							"session", sessionKey)
320						deps.MsgBus.PublishOutbound(bus.OutboundMessage{
321							Channel:  msg.Channel,
322							ChatID:   msg.ChatID,
323							Content:  i18n.T(locale, i18n.MsgInjectedAck),
324							Metadata: outMeta,
325						})
326						return
327					}
328					// Fallback: injection failed (channel full) → fall through to scheduler queue
329					slog.Info("inbound: steer injection failed, queueing as normal",
330						"session", sessionKey)
331				case agent.IntentNewTask:
332					// New unrelated request: fall through to scheduler queue
333					slog.Info("inbound: new task queued behind active run",
334						"session", sessionKey)
335				}
336			}
337		}
338	
339		// Inject tenant context from channel instance so all store queries are tenant-scoped.
340		if msg.TenantID != uuid.Nil {
341			ctx = store.WithTenantID(ctx, msg.TenantID)
342		}
343	
344		// Inject post-turn dispatch tracker so team task creates are deferred.
345		ptd := tools.NewPendingTeamDispatch()
346		schedCtx := tools.WithPendingTeamDispatch(ctx, ptd)
347	
348		// Propagate run_kind from metadata (e.g. "notification" for team task status relays).
349		if rk := msg.Metadata["run_kind"]; rk != "" {
350			schedCtx = tools.WithRunKind(schedCtx, rk)
351		}
352	
353		// Schedule through main lane (per-session concurrency controlled by maxConcurrent)
354		outCh := deps.Sched.ScheduleWithOpts(schedCtx, "main", agent.RunRequest{
355			SessionKey:        sessionKey,
356			Message:           msg.Content,
357			Media:             reqMedia,
358			ForwardMedia:      fwdMedia,
359			Channel:           msg.Channel,
360			ChannelType:       resolveChannelType(deps.ChannelMgr, msg.Channel),
361			ChatTitle:         msg.Metadata[tools.MetaChatTitle],
362			ChatID:            msg.ChatID,
363			PeerKind:          peerKind,
364			LocalKey:          msg.Metadata["local_key"],
365			UserID:            userID,
366			SenderID:          msg.SenderID,
367			SenderName:        resolveSenderName(msg),
368			RunID:             runID,
369			Stream:            enableStream,
370			HistoryLimit:      msg.HistoryLimit,
371			ToolAllow:         msg.ToolAllow,
372			ExtraSystemPrompt: extraPrompt,
373			SkillFilter:       skillFilter,
374		}, scheduler.ScheduleOpts{
375			MaxConcurrent: maxConcurrent,
376		})
377	
378		// Handle result asynchronously to not block the flush callback.
379		go func(agentKey, channel, chatID, session, rID, peerKind, inboundContent string, meta map[string]string, blockReplyEnabled bool, ptd *tools.PendingTeamDispatch) {
380			outcome := <-outCh
381	
382			// Release team create lock — tasks already visible in DB, other goroutines can list.
383			ptd.ReleaseTeamLock()
384	
385			// Post-turn: dispatch pending team tasks created during this turn.
386			if deps.PostTurn != nil {
387				for teamID, taskIDs := range ptd.Drain() {
388					if err := deps.PostTurn.ProcessPendingTasks(ctx, teamID, taskIDs); err != nil {
389						slog.Warn("post_turn: failed", "team_id", teamID, "error", err)
390					}
391				}
392			}
393	
394			// Clean up run tracking (in case HandleAgentEvent didn't fire for terminal events)
395			if deps.ChannelMgr != nil {
396				deps.ChannelMgr.UnregisterRun(rID)
397			}
398	
399			if outcome.Err != nil {
400				// Don't send error for cancelled runs (/stop command) —
401				// publish empty outbound to clean up thinking/typing indicators.
402				if errors.Is(outcome.Err, context.Canceled) {
403					slog.Info("inbound: run cancelled", "channel", channel, "session", session)
404					deps.MsgBus.PublishOutbound(bus.OutboundMessage{
405						Channel:  channel,
406						ChatID:   chatID,
407						Content:  "",
408						Metadata: meta,
409					})
410					return
411				}
412				slog.Error("inbound: agent run failed", "error", outcome.Err, "channel", channel)
413				deps.MsgBus.PublishOutbound(bus.OutboundMessage{
414					Channel:  channel,
415					ChatID:   chatID,
416					Content:  formatAgentError(outcome.Err),
417					Metadata: meta,
418				})
419				return
420			}
421	
422			// Suppress empty/NO_REPLY responses (matching TS normalize-reply.ts).
423			// Still publish an empty outbound so channels can clean up placeholder/thinking indicators.
424			if outcome.Result.Content == "" || agent.IsSilentReply(outcome.Result.Content) {
425				slog.Info("inbound: suppressed silent/empty reply",
426					"channel", channel,
427					"chat_id", chatID,
428					"session", session,
429				)
430				deps.MsgBus.PublishOutbound(bus.OutboundMessage{
431					Channel:  channel,
432					ChatID:   chatID,
433					Content:  "",
434					Metadata: meta,
435				})
436				return
437			}
438	
439			// Dedup: if block replies were delivered and the final content matches the last
440			// block reply, suppress the final message to avoid duplicate delivery.
441			// Only applies when blockReply is enabled (otherwise nothing was delivered).
442			if blockReplyEnabled && outcome.Result.BlockReplies > 0 && outcome.Result.Content == outcome.Result.LastBlockReply && len(outcome.Result.Media) == 0 {
443				slog.Debug("inbound: dedup final message (matches last block reply)",
444					"channel", channel, "run_id", rID)
445				deps.MsgBus.PublishOutbound(bus.OutboundMessage{
446					Channel:  channel,
447					ChatID:   chatID,
448					Content:  "",
449					Metadata: meta,
450				})
451				return
452			}
453	
454			// Sanitize voice agent replies: replace technical errors with user-friendly fallback.
455			replyContent := voiceguard.SanitizeReply(
456				deps.Cfg.Channels.Telegram.VoiceAgentID, agentKey,
457				channel, peerKind, inboundContent, outcome.Result.Content,
458				deps.Cfg.Channels.Telegram.AudioGuardFallbackTranscript,
459				deps.Cfg.Channels.Telegram.AudioGuardFallbackNoTranscript,
460				deps.Cfg.Channels.Telegram.AudioGuardErrorMarkers,
461			)
462	
463			// Publish response back to the channel

1	package facebook
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"log/slog"
6		"strings"
7	)
8	
9	// handleCommentEvent processes a feed webhook change where item == "comment".
10	func (ch *Channel) handleCommentEvent(entry WebhookEntry, change ChangeValue) {
11		// Feature gate.
12		if !ch.config.Features.CommentReply {
13			return
14		}
15	
16		// Only process new comments (not edits or deletions).
17		if change.Verb != "add" {
18			return
19		}
20	
21		// Page routing: ensure this event belongs to our page instance (before dedup write).
22		if entry.ID != ch.pageID {
23			return
24		}
25	
26		// Self-reply prevention: skip comments posted by the page itself.
27		if change.From.ID == ch.pageID {
28			return
29		}
30	
31		// Dedup: Facebook may deliver the same event more than once.
32		if ch.isDup("comment:" + change.CommentID) {
33			slog.Debug("facebook: duplicate comment event skipped", "comment_id", change.CommentID)
34			return
35		}
36	
37		// Build message content — optionally enriched with post + thread context.
38		// Use stopCtx so inflight Graph API calls are cancelled when the channel stops.
39		content := change.Message
40		if ch.config.CommentReplyOptions.IncludePostContext && change.PostID != "" {
41			content = ch.buildEnrichedContent(change)
42		}
43	
44		senderID := change.From.ID
45		// Session key groups all comments by the same user on the same post.
46		chatID := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", change.PostID, senderID)
47	
48		metadata := map[string]string{
49			"comment_id":          change.CommentID,
50			"post_id":             change.PostID,
51			"parent_id":           change.ParentID,
52			"sender_name":         change.From.Name,
53			"sender_id":           senderID,
54			"fb_mode":             "comment",
55			"reply_to_comment_id": change.CommentID,
56		}
57	
58		ch.HandleMessage(senderID, chatID, content, nil, metadata, "direct")
59	}
60	
61	// buildEnrichedContent fetches post content and comment thread, assembles a rich
62	// context string for the agent. Uses stopCtx so it is cancelled on channel Stop().
63	func (ch *Channel) buildEnrichedContent(change ChangeValue) string {
64		ctx := ch.stopCtx
65	
66		var sb strings.Builder
67	
68		// Post content.
69		post, err := ch.postFetcher.GetPost(ctx, change.PostID)
70		if err == nil && post != nil && post.Message != "" {
71			sb.WriteString("[Bài đăng] ")
72			sb.WriteString(post.Message)
73			sb.WriteString("\n\n")
74		}
75	
76		// Comment thread (when this is a nested reply).
77		if change.ParentID != "" {
78			depth := ch.config.CommentReplyOptions.MaxThreadDepth
79			if depth <= 0 {
80				depth = 10
81			}
82			thread, err := ch.postFetcher.GetCommentThread(ctx, change.ParentID, depth)
83			if err == nil && len(thread) > 0 {
84				sb.WriteString("[Thread]\n")
85				for _, c := range thread {
86					sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- %s: %s\n", c.From.Name, c.Message))
87				}
88				sb.WriteString("\n")
89			}
90		}
91	
92		// Current comment.
93		sb.WriteString("[Comment mới] ")
94		sb.WriteString(change.From.Name)
95		sb.WriteString(": ")
96		sb.WriteString(change.Message)
97	
98		return sb.String()
99	}

content.text:
Implemented the Pancake COMMENT webhook support.

Summary:
- Routed Pancake `COMMENT` webhook events instead of dropping all non-`INBOX` events.
- Added `handleCommentEvent()` with:
  - `features.comment_reply` gate
  - self/staff reply prevention
  - message/comment dedup
  - keyword/all filtering via `comment_reply_options.filter` + `keywords`
  - optional post context enrichment
  - metadata for downstream reply routing (`pancake_mode=comment`, `reply_to_comment_id`, etc.)
- Added Pancake comment send path:
  - `reply_comment` action for comment replies
  - one-time `private_reply` first-inbox DM when `features.first_inbox` is enabled
- Added Pancake `PostFetcher` using `singleflight` and TTL cache over list-all-posts.
- Extended Pancake config/types for:
  - `features.first_inbox`
  - `comment_reply_options`
  - `post_context_cache_ttl`
  - `first_inbox_message`
- Added API client methods:
  - `ReplyComment`
  - `PrivateReply`
  - `ListPosts`

Validation:
- Wrote temporary regression tests to reproduce:
  - COMMENT webhook routing
  - `reply_comment` + one-time `private_reply`
- Confirmed they failed before/while implementing, then passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test edits as requested.
- Ran final targeted tests:

```bash
go test ./internal/channels/pancake
```

Result: passing.

Note:
- I did not commit changes.
- Existing untracked `reports/` remains untouched.
